message:

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Unit tests:
  - test_load_builtin_preset() - audit preset loads correctly

  - test_preset_override_by_cli() - CLI flags win over preset
  - test_multiple_presets_merge() - later preset overrides earlier

- E2E test script (tests/e2e/presets.sh):

  - Create custom preset, verify it loads
  - Verify CLI flag overrides preset value
  - Test --list-presets output format
  - Log: preset content, effective config, actual behavior

- Documentation:
  - README section: 'Presets'
  - Table of built-in presets with descriptions
  - Example: creating custom preset
  - Troubleshooting: preset not found","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:05:58.076841992Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:03.125213272Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-23q","depends_on_id":"bd-26d","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:05:58.096911567Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-23q","depends_on_id":"bd-3t7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:03.125194958Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-26d","title":"Smart filtering presets","description":"Purpose:
- Reduce cognitive load of remembering many CLI flags
- Enable repeatable workflows with named configurations
- Make common use cases trivially easy

User impact:
- 'rano --preset audit' replaces 'rano --no-dns --log-file audit.log --json ...'
- Users define their own presets for team consistency
- Built-in presets cover common scenarios out of the box

Features:
- --preset <name> flag to load named configuration
- Preset search paths:
  1. ~/.config/rano/presets/<name>.conf
  2. /etc/rano/presets/<name>.conf
  3. Built-in presets (compiled in)
- Preset format: same as config.conf (key=value)
- CLI flags override preset values
- --list-presets to show available presets

Built-in presets:
- 'audit': json=true, log_dir=/tmp/rano-audit, no_dns=true, stats_interval_ms=0
- 'quiet': summary_only=true, stats_interval_ms=0, no_banner=true
- 'live': stats_interval_ms=1000, stats_width=80, no_sqlite=true
- 'verbose': show_ancestry=true, include_udp=true, include_listening=true

Testing:
- Unit tests for preset loading and override precedence
- E2E script that uses each built-in preset
- E2E script that creates custom preset and verifies loading","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:04:57.987573677Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:04:58.069192272Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-26d","depends_on_id":"bd-3o1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:04:58.067868850Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-291","title":"Add alert CLI flags and config options","description":"Deliverables:
- CLI flags added to argument parser:
  --alert-domain <pattern> (repeatable)
  --alert-max-connections <N>
  --alert-max-per-provider <N>
  --alert-duration-ms <N>
  --alert-unknown-domain
  --alert-bell
  --alert-cooldown-ms <N> (default 10000)

- Config file keys:
  alert_domain=pattern1,pattern2
  alert_max_connections=100
  alert_max_per_provider=50
  alert_duration_ms=30000
  alert_unknown_domain=true
  alert_bell=false
  alert_cooldown_ms=10000

- Help text for each flag with examples
- Validation: patterns must compile, numbers must be positive","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:44.520740196Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:10.138184362Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-291","depends_on_id":"bd-1jj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:10.138158894Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-291","depends_on_id":"bd-2iu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:44.549648598Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2a6","title":"Design export format and CLI","description":"Deliverables:
- CLI design document:
  rano export [OPTIONS]
  --format csv|jsonl (required)
  --sqlite <path> (default: observer.sqlite)
  --since <timestamp|relative> (e.g., 24h, 7d, 2026-01-20)
  --until <timestamp|relative>
  --run-id <id>
  --provider <name> (repeatable)
  --domain <pattern> (repeatable)
  --fields <list> (optional: override default field list)
  --no-header (CSV only: skip header row)

- Define field order and names:
  ts, run_id, event, provider, pid, comm, cmdline, proto, local_ip, local_port, remote_ip, remote_port, domain, duration_ms

- CSV format spec:
  - RFC 4180 compliant
  - Fields with commas/quotes/newlines properly escaped
  - UTF-8 encoding
  - CRLF line endings (for Excel compatibility)

- JSONL format spec:
  - One JSON object per line
  - UTF-8 encoding
  - LF line endings
  - Same fields as CSV

- Output: docs/export-design.md","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:32.336206689Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:32.364424220Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2a6","depends_on_id":"bd-1vz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:32.364391899Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-2a6","title":"Design export format and CLI","description":"Deliverables:
- CLI design document:
  rano export [OPTIONS]
  --format csv|jsonl (required)
  --sqlite <path> (default: observer.sqlite)
  --since <timestamp|relative> (e.g., 24h, 7d, 2026-01-20)
  --until <timestamp|relative>
  --run-id <id>
  --provider <name> (repeatable)
  --domain <pattern> (repeatable)
  --fields <list> (optional: override default field list)
  --no-header (CSV only: skip header row)

- Define field order and names:
  ts, run_id, event, provider, pid, comm, cmdline, proto, local_ip, local_port, remote_ip, remote_port, domain, duration_ms

- CSV format spec:
  - RFC 4180 compliant
  - Fields with commas/quotes/newlines properly escaped
  - UTF-8 encoding
  - CRLF line endings (for Excel compatibility)

- JSONL format spec:
  - One JSON object per line
  - UTF-8 encoding
  - LF line endings
  - Same fields as CSV

- Output: docs/export-design.md","design":"Implementation notes:
- CSV escaping: double quotes to escape quotes, wrap fields containing comma/quote/newline
- No external CSV crate needed - simple escaping logic
- Streaming: use SQLite row iteration with step(), don't collect()
- Memory: flush stdout every 1000 rows
- Empty result: CSV outputs header only, JSONL outputs nothing
- Progress: no progress bar (would interfere with piping)
- Errors: fail fast on invalid filter, print to stderr

Query template:
SELECT ts, run_id, event, provider, pid, comm, cmdline, proto,
       local_ip, local_port, remote_ip, remote_port, domain, duration_ms
FROM events
WHERE 1=1
  [AND ts >= :since]
  [AND ts < :until]
  [AND run_id = :run_id]
  [AND provider IN (:providers)]
  [AND domain LIKE :domain_pattern]
ORDER BY ts ASC","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:32.336206689Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:13:59.423768123Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2a6","depends_on_id":"bd-1vz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:32.364391899Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fm","title":"Alert tests and documentation","description":"Deliverables:
- Unit tests:
  - test_alert_domain_pattern_match() - glob and regex patterns
  - test_alert_max_connections_threshold() - trigger at exact threshold
  - test_alert_cooldown_prevents_spam() - no re-alert within cooldown
  - test_alert_duration_threshold() - long-running connections flagged
  - test_alert_unknown_domain() - unresolved domains trigger alert
  - test_alert_config_validation() - invalid patterns rejected

- E2E test script (tests/e2e/alert-thresholds.sh):
  - Use mock process that makes N connections
  - Verify domain watch alert triggers on matching domain
  - Verify max-connections alert at threshold
  - Verify cooldown prevents duplicate alerts
  - Verify alerts appear in stderr, not stdout
  - Verify SQLite has alert=true for alert events
  - Log: inputs, expected alerts, actual alerts

- Documentation:
  - README section: 'Alert Thresholds'
  - Example use cases: security audit, rate limit detection
  - Troubleshooting: 'why aren't my alerts firing'","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:03.601295333Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:11.080956242Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fm","depends_on_id":"bd-2iu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:03.619668324Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2fm","depends_on_id":"bd-lr2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:11.080924402Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2iu","title":"Alert thresholds and watch mode","description":"Purpose:
- Transform passive monitoring into active alerting
- Allow users to set guard rails and not watch output constantly
- Enable security-focused workflows with domain watching

User impact:
- 'rano --alert-domain evil.com --alert-max-connections 100' instantly flags suspicious activity
- Auditors can set thresholds and walk away
- Alerts are prominent and logged separately

Features:
- --alert-domain <pattern>: Regex/glob for domains to highlight/warn
- --alert-max-connections <N>: Warn when total connections exceed N
- --alert-max-per-provider <N>: Warn when any provider exceeds N
- --alert-duration-ms <N>: Flag connections lasting longer than N ms
- --alert-unknown-domain: Warn on any connection to unresolved domain

Output behavior:
- Alerts print to stderr with [ALERT] prefix
- Alerts use distinct color (red/yellow)
- Optional --alert-bell to ring terminal bell
- Alert events logged to SQLite with alert=true flag

Testing:
- Unit tests for threshold checking and pattern matching
- E2E script that triggers each alert type and validates output
- E2E script must verify alerts don't fire for normal traffic

Constraints:
- Must not add latency to main polling loop
- Patterns must be case-insensitive and support wildcards","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:19.697313595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:19.713783963Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2iu","depends_on_id":"bd-3o1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:19.713686680Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2xz","title":"Implement session name generation","description":"Deliverables:
- generate_session_name(patterns: &[String], primary_provider: Provider) -> String:
  - time_of_day: night/morning/afternoon/evening
  - provider: anthropic/openai/google/mixed
  - pattern: first pattern or 'default'
  - date: YYYY-MM-DD
  - Format: '{time}-{provider}-{pattern}-{date}'

- CLI: --session-name <name> to override
- Store in sessions table: session_name column
- Report accepts --session <name> as filter (in addition to --run-id)

- Unit tests for name generation logic
- E2E test verifying session name in report output","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:11:22.010801099Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:11:22.087944838Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2xz","depends_on_id":"bd-q9u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:11:22.086836020Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
@@ -20,8 +20,8 @@
 {"id":"bd-36b","title":"Shell prompt integration","description":"Purpose:
- Provide one-line status for embedding in shell prompts (PS1, starship)
- Always-visible network status without dedicating a terminal

User impact:
- Prompt shows: '🔵 3 active | anthropic:45 openai:12'
- Instant awareness of AI network activity
- Configurable format for different prompt systems

Features:
- rano status --one-line: output for prompt embedding
- rano status --format <template>: custom format string
- Fast execution (<50ms) for prompt responsiveness
- Read from SQLite or shared state file

Template variables:
- {active}: current active connection count
- {total}: total connections this session
- {anthropic}, {openai}, {google}: per-provider counts
- {last_domain}: most recent domain
- {session_name}: current session name

Default format: '{active} active | anthropic:{anthropic} openai:{openai}'

Implementation:
- Query latest session from SQLite
- Cache results for 1s to avoid repeated queries
- Optional daemon mode for even faster response

Testing:
- Unit tests for template expansion
- E2E script verifying output format
- Benchmark: must complete in <50ms","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:07:36.403153804Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:07:36.420956080Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-36b","depends_on_id":"bd-3o1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:07:36.420930913Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3dy","title":"Implement retry detection logic","description":"Deliverables:
- RetryTracker struct:
  - recent: HashMap<(IpAddr, u16, u32), Vec<Instant>>
  - threshold: usize (default 3)
  - window_ms: u64 (default 60000)

- track_connection(key: &ConnKey, pid: u32) -> Option<RetryWarning>:
  - Add current timestamp to Vec for (ip, port, pid)
  - Prune entries older than window_ms
  - If count >= threshold, return warning

- RetryWarning struct:
  - count: usize
  - window_seconds: f64
  - endpoint: (IpAddr, u16)

- Integration: call after close event detection","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:09:44.229045587Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:09:44.287869715Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3dy","depends_on_id":"bd-1i6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:09:44.287016248Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3o1","title":"rano v2: alerting, export, UX improvements","description":"Goals:
- Add proactive alerting capabilities for security monitoring
- Enable data export for integration with external tools
- Improve UX with presets, ancestry display, and accessibility
- Reduce friction through config validation and session naming

Context:
- Current rano is observation-only; users must watch output constantly
- SQLite data is locked inside; no easy export to CSV/Pandas/grep
- Config debugging requires trial-and-error runs
- Session IDs are opaque (PID-timestamp) not human-readable

Scope:
- 5 prioritized features with comprehensive tests and documentation
- Each feature is independent but builds on existing infrastructure
- Preserve all existing CLI behavior; improvements are additive

Quality bar:
- Every feature must include unit tests and at least one e2e test script
- E2E scripts must use the existing harness (scripts/e2e/lib.sh)
- Tests must run offline without elevated privileges
- Documentation updates for README and --help text

Acceptance:
- Each child feature has tasks with unit tests + e2e scripts + docs
- All new flags documented in README
- Config validation catches common errors before runtime","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:06.726854553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:06.726854553Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-3t7","title":"Implement preset loading","description":"Deliverables:
- PresetLoader struct:
  - builtin_presets: HashMap<&'static str, &'static str> (name -> content)
  - user_preset_dirs: Vec<PathBuf>

- load_preset(name) -> Result<HashMap<String, String>>:
  1. Check builtin_presets first
  2. Search user_preset_dirs for <name>.conf
  3. Parse as key=value, return HashMap

- Integration with config loading:
  1. Parse --preset flags early
  2. For each preset (in order):
     - Load preset config
     - Merge into base config
  3. Apply remaining CLI flags (override)

- list_presets() -> Vec<PresetInfo>:
  - Scan builtin + user dirs
  - Extract description from first line comment (# Description: ...)
  - Format for --list-presets output

- Built-in preset contents (as static strings):
  - PRESET_AUDIT, PRESET_QUIET, PRESET_LIVE, PRESET_VERBOSE","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:05:49.958498465Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:02.835758525Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3t7","depends_on_id":"bd-26d","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:05:49.987421243Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3t7","depends_on_id":"bd-db8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:02.835734189Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-52z","title":"Implement config validation logic","description":"Deliverables:
- ConfigValidator struct:
  - errors: Vec<ConfigError>
  - warnings: Vec<ConfigWarning>

- validate_config_file(path) -> ValidationResult:
  - Parse as key=value
  - Check each key against known_keys set
  - Validate value types per key
  - Compile patterns to catch regex errors

- validate_toml_config(path) -> ValidationResult:
  - Parse as TOML
  - Check [providers] section structure
  - Validate mode is 'merge' or 'replace'
  - Check pattern arrays are strings

- validate_paths(config) -> ValidationResult:
  - log_dir: directory exists or can be created
  - sqlite: parent directory exists
  - config files: exist if explicitly specified

- validate_patterns(config) -> ValidationResult:
  - Compile all pattern/exclude_pattern as regex/glob
  - Report syntax errors with pattern text

- ValidationResult:
  - is_valid: bool (no errors)
  - errors: Vec<(file, line, message)>
  - warnings: Vec<(file, line, message)>","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:21.748149071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:21.762544140Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-52z","depends_on_id":"bd-n2d","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:21.762516839Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-3t7","title":"Implement preset loading","description":"Deliverables:
- PresetLoader struct:
  - builtin_presets: HashMap<&'static str, &'static str> (name -> content)
  - user_preset_dirs: Vec<PathBuf>

- load_preset(name) -> Result<HashMap<String, String>>:
  1. Check builtin_presets first
  2. Search user_preset_dirs for <name>.conf
  3. Parse as key=value, return HashMap

- Integration with config loading:
  1. Parse --preset flags early
  2. For each preset (in order):
     - Load preset config
     - Merge into base config
  3. Apply remaining CLI flags (override)

- list_presets() -> Vec<PresetInfo>:
  - Scan builtin + user dirs
  - Extract description from first line comment (# Description: ...)
  - Format for --list-presets output

- Built-in preset contents (as static strings):
  - PRESET_AUDIT, PRESET_QUIET, PRESET_LIVE, PRESET_VERBOSE","design":"Built-in preset definitions:

# audit.conf - Security auditing workflow
# Description: Full logging for security audits
json=true
log_dir=/tmp/rano-audit
no_dns=false
stats_interval_ms=0
show_ancestry=true

# quiet.conf - Minimal output
# Description: Summary only, no live output
summary_only=true
stats_interval_ms=0
no_banner=true

# live.conf - Real-time monitoring focus
# Description: Frequent stats, no SQLite overhead
stats_interval_ms=1000
stats_width=80
no_sqlite=true
stats_view=domain

# verbose.conf - Maximum detail
# Description: All information, all protocols
show_ancestry=true
include_udp=true
include_listening=true
stats_interval_ms=2000

Search order:
1. ~/.config/rano/presets/<name>.conf
2. /etc/rano/presets/<name>.conf (system-wide)
3. Built-in (compiled in)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:05:49.958498465Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:15:24.807812100Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3t7","depends_on_id":"bd-26d","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:05:49.987421243Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3t7","depends_on_id":"bd-db8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:02.835734189Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-52z","title":"Implement config validation logic","description":"Deliverables:
- ConfigValidator struct:
  - errors: Vec<ConfigError>
  - warnings: Vec<ConfigWarning>

- validate_config_file(path) -> ValidationResult:
  - Parse as key=value
  - Check each key against known_keys set
  - Validate value types per key
  - Compile patterns to catch regex errors

- validate_toml_config(path) -> ValidationResult:
  - Parse as TOML
  - Check [providers] section structure
  - Validate mode is 'merge' or 'replace'
  - Check pattern arrays are strings

- validate_paths(config) -> ValidationResult:
  - log_dir: directory exists or can be created
  - sqlite: parent directory exists
  - config files: exist if explicitly specified

- validate_patterns(config) -> ValidationResult:
  - Compile all pattern/exclude_pattern as regex/glob
  - Report syntax errors with pattern text

- ValidationResult:
  - is_valid: bool (no errors)
  - errors: Vec<(file, line, message)>
  - warnings: Vec<(file, line, message)>","design":"Known config keys (for typo detection):
# Process selection
pattern, exclude_pattern, pid, no_descendants

# Polling
interval_ms, once

# Output
json, summary_only, color, log_file, log_dir, log_format, no_color, no_banner, theme

# Domain resolution
no_dns, domain_mode, pcap, include_udp, no_udp, include_listening

# SQLite
sqlite, no_sqlite, db_batch_size, db_flush_ms, db_queue_max

# Stats
stats_interval_ms, stats_width, stats_top, stats_view, stats_cycle_ms

# Alerts (new)
alert_domain, alert_max_connections, alert_max_per_provider, alert_duration_ms, alert_unknown_domain, alert_bell, alert_cooldown_ms

# Presets (new)
preset

Value type validation:
- _ms, _size, _max, _top, _width: positive integer
- pattern, exclude_pattern: compilable regex/glob
- color, log_format, domain_mode, theme: enum values
- Boolean keys: true/false","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:21.748149071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:14:37.027444957Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-52z","depends_on_id":"bd-n2d","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:21.762516839Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-90z","title":"Retry detection CLI and tests","description":"Deliverables:
- CLI flags: --retry-threshold <N>, --retry-window-ms <N>
- Config keys: retry_threshold, retry_window_ms
- Warning output format: '⚠ Retry pattern: N connections to IP:port in Xs'
- SQLite: add retry_count column to events (for close events)

- Unit tests:
  - test_retry_detection_triggers_at_threshold()
  - test_retry_detection_window_expiry()
  - test_retry_detection_per_endpoint()

- E2E test: simulate rapid reconnects, verify warnings","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:10:15.710962523Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:10:49.214866797Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-90z","depends_on_id":"bd-1i6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:10:15.753292539Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-90z","depends_on_id":"bd-3dy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:10:49.214275153Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-94z","title":"Ancestry tests and documentation","description":"Deliverables:
- Unit tests:
  - test_read_ancestry_single_level() - process with no parent
  - test_read_ancestry_multi_level() - 3+ level chain
  - test_ancestry_cache_hit() - cached value returned
  - test_ancestry_cache_staleness() - recompute on comm mismatch
  - test_format_ancestry_truncation() - >5 levels truncated

- E2E test script (tests/e2e/process-ancestry.sh):

 {"id":"bd-n2d","title":"Config validation command","description":"Purpose:
- Catch configuration errors before runtime
- Reduce debugging time for 'why isn't my config working'
- Provide clear feedback on config file issues

User impact:
- 'rano config check' validates all config files and reports issues
- 'rano config show' displays resolved configuration
- Clear error messages point to exact file:line of problems

Features:
- rano config check: validate config.conf, rano.toml, presets
- rano config show: display resolved config as key=value
- rano config show --json: display as JSON for automation
- rano config paths: show where config files are loaded from

Validation checks:
- Syntax: valid key=value, valid TOML
- Known keys: warn on unknown keys (typo detection)
- Value types: numbers parse, booleans valid
- Patterns: regex/glob patterns compile
- Paths: referenced files exist (log_dir, sqlite)
- Provider config: patterns are valid, mode is merge/replace

Exit codes:
- 0: valid configuration
- 1: errors found (won't run)
- 2: warnings only (will run but may have issues)

Testing:
- Unit tests for each validation check
- E2E script with invalid configs to verify error messages","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:13.564851300Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:13.594283838Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-n2d","depends_on_id":"bd-3o1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:13.594253010Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-q9u","title":"Automatic session naming","description":"Purpose:
- Generate human-readable session names instead of opaque run_ids
- Enable 'remember that session last Tuesday' conversations

User impact:
- Sessions named like 'morning-claude-audit-2026-01-20' instead of '12345-1705512345000'
- 'rano report --session morning-claude-audit-2026-01-20' for lookup
- Easier to discuss sessions in team contexts

Features:
- Auto-generate name on session start: {time_of_day}-{primary_provider}-{pattern}-{date}
- --session-name <name> to override auto-generated name
- Store in sessions table alongside run_id
- Report and export accept session name as filter

Time of day mapping:
- 00:00-06:00: night
- 06:00-12:00: morning
- 12:00-18:00: afternoon
- 18:00-24:00: evening

Testing:
- Unit tests for name generation
- E2E script verifying names appear in report
- Test custom --session-name override","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:07:05.697380819Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:07:05.727882240Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-q9u","depends_on_id":"bd-3o1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:07:05.727782292Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

- E2E test script (tests/e2e/export-formats.sh):

  - Export to CSV, validate header and row count
  - Export to JSONL, validate each line is valid JSON
  - Test --since/--until filters
  - Test --provider filter
  - Test --domain filter

  - Log: filter inputs, row counts, sample output

- Documentation:

-{"id":"bd-vb7","title":"Implement ancestry chain reading","description":"Deliverables:
- read_ancestry(pid) -> Vec<(u32, String)>:
  - Walk /proc/{pid}/stat to get PPID
  - Recursively read parent until PPID=0 or PPID=1
  - Return chain from root to current: [(init_pid, init_comm), ..., (pid, comm)]

- AncestryCache struct:
  - cache: HashMap<u32, (Vec<(u32, String)>, Instant)>
  - ttl: Duration (default 30s)
  - get_or_compute(pid) method with staleness check

- Staleness detection:
  - If cached entry exists, verify current comm matches cached comm
  - If mismatch (PID recycled), invalidate and recompute

- format_ancestry(chain) -> String:
  - Output: 'zsh(123) → tmux(456) → claude(789)'
  - Truncate if >5 levels: '... → parent(456) → claude(789)'
  - Handle single-level: 'claude(789)'

- Integration:
  - Add ancestry_cache to main state
  - Lookup ancestry when --show-ancestry enabled
  - Pass ancestry to event formatting","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:02:49.224824805Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:02:49.295706371Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vb7","depends_on_id":"bd-1ju","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:02:49.294596381Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-vb7","title":"Implement ancestry chain reading","description":"Deliverables:
- read_ancestry(pid) -> Vec<(u32, String)>:
  - Walk /proc/{pid}/stat to get PPID
  - Recursively read parent until PPID=0 or PPID=1
  - Return chain from root to current: [(init_pid, init_comm), ..., (pid, comm)]

- AncestryCache struct:
  - cache: HashMap<u32, (Vec<(u32, String)>, Instant)>
  - ttl: Duration (default 30s)
  - get_or_compute(pid) method with staleness check

- Staleness detection:
  - If cached entry exists, verify current comm matches cached comm
  - If mismatch (PID recycled), invalidate and recompute

- format_ancestry(chain) -> String:
  - Output: 'zsh(123) → tmux(456) → claude(789)'
  - Truncate if >5 levels: '... → parent(456) → claude(789)'
  - Handle single-level: 'claude(789)'

- Integration:
  - Add ancestry_cache to main state
  - Lookup ancestry when --show-ancestry enabled
  - Pass ancestry to event formatting","design":"Implementation approach:
- Reuse existing read_ppid() from descendant tracking
- Stack-based iteration (not recursion) to avoid stack overflow
- Stop at PPID=0 (kernel) or PPID=1 (init/systemd)
- Reverse to get root→leaf order

Performance considerations:
- Cache is essential - ancestry changes rarely
- 30s TTL is reasonable (processes don't typically reparent)
- Staleness check: comm is sufficient (PID reuse with same comm is rare)
- Memory: ~100 bytes per cached entry, max ~1000 tracked PIDs = 100KB

Edge cases:
- Process exits during walk: return partial chain with '?' for missing
- Zombie process: use comm from cmdline fallback
- Init containers: stop at container init, not host init","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:02:49.224824805Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:14:14.720272034Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vb7","depends_on_id":"bd-1ju","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:02:49.294596381Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w","title":"rano vNext: accuracy + insight + performance","description":"Goals:
- Make domain attribution accurate and trustworthy when possible.
- Make SQLite logging obviously useful through reporting and live insights.
- Improve performance and reliability under high event rates.
- Keep UX simple: defaults work, advanced options are discoverable.

Context:
- Current capture is PTR-only and can misattribute domains.
- SQLite has strong schema/views but no first-class reporting UX.
- Event rates can spike and cause sqlite overhead.
- Provider mapping is fixed to substring matching; users need overrides.

Scope:
- Implement 5 prioritized improvements with tasks, tests, and docs.
- Preserve existing CLI behavior; improvements are additive.

Quality bar:
- Every feature must include comprehensive unit tests and at least one e2e test script.
- E2E scripts must include detailed, human-readable logging (inputs, expected outputs, actual outputs).
- Tests must run offline without elevated privileges unless explicitly marked otherwise.

Acceptance:
- Each child epic has tasks with unit tests + e2e scripts + docs.
- All new features degrade gracefully (no sudo/libpcap, missing config).
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-17T22:00:47.256141412Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-17T22:03:44.518182522Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.1","title":"True domain attribution via libpcap DNS+SNI","description":"Purpose:
- Provide accurate hostname attribution via DNS responses and TLS SNI, not just PTR.
- Preserve current PTR fallback when libpcap/sudo is unavailable.

User impact:
- Domains match what the client intended (SNI), not a reverse DNS guess.
- Clear warnings when privileged capture is unavailable; no silent failure.

Constraints:
- Must be feature-gated to avoid new deps for default builds.
- Must avoid any breaking change to default CLI behavior.

Testing:
- Unit tests for DNS/SNI parsing + mapping logic.
- E2E script using offline pcap fixtures to validate domain attribution and fallback logging.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-17T22:00:47.293260146Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-17T22:03:44.555701291Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.1","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T04:55:47Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.1.1","title":"Design pcap dataflow + mapping model","description":"Deliverables:
- Dataflow diagram of packet capture -> DNS/SNI extraction -> IP/port mapping -> event enrichment.
- Decision on retention/TTL for SNI/DNS mappings.
- Clear rules for IPv4/IPv6 and UDP/TCP handling.

Notes:
- Aim for minimal parsing to avoid heavy CPU usage.
- Prefer map keyed by (remote_ip, remote_port) with TTL to avoid staleness.
","design":"-","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-17T22:00:47.330068504Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T02:35:57.065011635Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T02:35:57.065011635Z","close_reason":"Design document created at docs/pcap-design.md covering dataflow, DNS/SNI parsing, cache TTL, IPv4/IPv6, and feature gating.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.1.1","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-35w.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T04:55:47Z","created_by":"import"}]}
